title: Privileged User Activity Monitoring

compliance.assurance_rationale: Monitors privileged and identity-management actions in Catalyst Center to support least-privilege review and incident detection.
compliance.controlObjective: Surfaces who exercises powerful accounts and identity controls on the management system, helping enforce NIST 800-53 AC-6 (least privilege) in practice through detective review.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Dashboard or alert `UC-5.13.47` with monthly privileged-activity printout and reviewer sign-off for access governance.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Retention of auditable events for high-risk administrative actions on Catalyst Center for security monitoring.
compliance.controlObjective: Confirms the auditable event stream includes privileged-identity and role-management work needed for log review (AU-2) on the control plane.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Same saved search, tied to the SIEM or log-management retention schedule and sampling notes for the audit function.

dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:audit:logs`. from the `audit_logs` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/data/api/v1/event/event-series/audit-log`. Default poll interval: 300s (5m). Volume: Variable; ~10-100 events/day in typical environments. RBAC: SUPER-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `auditUserName` (string), `auditRequestType` (string), `auditDescription` (string), `auditParentId` (string), `createdDateTime` (epoch milliseconds), `isSystemEvent` (boolean). CIM data model: Change.
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:audit:logs" (auditUserName="admin" OR auditRequestType="*USER*" OR auditRequestType="*ROLE*" OR auditRequestType="*PERMISSION*") | stats count as action_count dc(auditRequestType) as unique_actions values(auditRequestType) as action_types by auditUserName, auditIpAddress | sort -action_count
description: Monitors activity from privileged accounts (admins, super-admins) and user/role management actions to detect potential abuse or compromised credentials.
value: Privileged accounts can cause maximum damage. Monitoring their activity detects unauthorized use, credential compromise, and insider threats.
implementation: Enable the `audit_logs` input in the Cisco Catalyst TA pointing to `index=catalyst`. The TA polls audit log data from the Catalyst Center Intent API `/dna/intent/api/v1/audit/logs` every 5 minutes. Key fields: `auditRequestType`, `auditDescription`, `auditUserName`, `auditTimestamp`, `auditIpAddress`.
visualization: Table (auditUserName, auditIpAddress, action_count, unique_actions, action_types), alert on spikes or new admin sources.

references.title: Catalyst Center Audit Log API ‚Äî Cisco DevNet

knownFalsePositives:
**Planned break-glass access for emergency maintenance.** During critical outages, administrators may use elevated privileges for emergency access, generating privileged user audit entries. These are legitimate authorized activities. Distinguish by correlating with ITSM emergency change records or on-call schedules. Suppress by annotating privileged activity with approved emergency change ticket numbers.

**IdP role synchronization pushing role updates to Catalyst Center.** When the Identity Provider (Okta, Azure AD, etc.) syncs role assignments, Catalyst Center logs role change events for all affected users. This can generate a burst of USER and ROLE audit entries. Distinguish by checking whether multiple role changes occur within seconds and correlate with IdP sync schedules. Suppress by filtering IdP-sourced role changes from the anomaly detection and tracking them as bulk IAM operations.

**Periodic access review generating audit entries when reviewers confirm or adjust roles.** During scheduled access reviews, reviewers may confirm or modify role assignments for multiple users, generating a cluster of privileged activity entries. Distinguish by correlating with access review schedules. Suppress by allowing access review periods as known high-activity windows.

**Service account used for API automation flagged as privileged activity.** Service accounts with SUPER-ADMIN or NETWORK-ADMIN roles generate audit entries for every API call. In automated environments, this can produce significant volume. Distinguish by checking whether the `username` corresponds to a known service account. Suppress by filtering service accounts from the human privileged activity dashboard using a `catalyst_service_accounts` lookup.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) with the **audit_logs** input enabled to `index=catalyst`, `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:audit:logs`.
‚Ä¢ RBAC: TA service account with **SUPER-ADMIN-ROLE** to access audit log API.
‚Ä¢ Define "privileged" operations for your organization: typically USER, ROLE, CREDENTIAL, and SUPER-ADMIN-ROLE resource types. Document the list of service accounts (automation, monitoring) to distinguish from human administrative activity.

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
The TA polls `GET /dna/data/api/v1/event/event-series/audit-log` at the configured interval. Privileged activity monitoring focuses on a subset of audit log entries that involve elevated operations.

Key fields for privileged user monitoring:
‚Ä¢ `username`: the administrator or service account performing the action.
‚Ä¢ `auditResourceType`: focus on "USER", "ROLE", "CREDENTIAL", "CERTIFICATE", "SYSTEM_SETTINGS".
‚Ä¢ `auditRequestType`: POST (create user/role), PUT (modify user/role), DELETE (remove user/role).
‚Ä¢ `auditDescription`: human-readable description of the privileged action.
‚Ä¢ `isSystemEvent`: true for system-initiated operations (exclude from human activity monitoring).
‚Ä¢ `sourceIP`: the IP address from which the administrator accessed Catalyst Center (if available in the audit entry).

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:audit:logs" auditResourceType IN ("USER","ROLE","CREDENTIAL","CERTIFICATE","SYSTEM_SETTINGS") isSystemEvent!=true
| stats count as action_count values(auditDescription) as actions values(auditRequestType) as request_types by username, auditResourceType
| sort -action_count
```

Understanding this SPL:
‚Ä¢ **`auditResourceType IN ("USER","ROLE","CREDENTIAL","CERTIFICATE","SYSTEM_SETTINGS")`**: Filters to only the resource types that represent privileged operations. Modify this list based on your organization's definition of "privileged."
‚Ä¢ **`isSystemEvent!=true`**: Excludes system-initiated operations (e.g., automatic certificate rotation) to focus on human administrator activity.
‚Ä¢ **`values(auditRequestType)`**: Shows whether the actions were create (POST), modify (PUT), or delete (DELETE) ‚Äî DELETE operations on USER or CREDENTIAL are highest priority.

For anomaly detection: `| eventstats avg(action_count) as baseline by username | eval ratio=action_count/baseline | where ratio > 3` ‚Äî flags users with 3x their normal privileged activity volume.

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
‚Ä¢ **Test event:** Create a test user in Catalyst Center > System > Users, then modify their role, then delete the user. Verify all three operations appear in Splunk with the correct `auditResourceType`, `username`, and `auditRequestType`.
‚Ä¢ **Count check:** Compare the privileged action count in Splunk with the Catalyst Center audit log filtered to USER/ROLE operations.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
‚Ä¢ **Dashboard:** Privileged activity timeline (timechart by `username`), recent privileged actions table with drill-down to details, and a single-value tile for "Privileged Actions Today" compared to the 7-day average.
‚Ä¢ **Alert:** Trigger on: (1) CREDENTIAL or CERTIFICATE DELETE operations, (2) ROLE changes granting SUPER-ADMIN-ROLE, (3) any USER/ROLE operations by an unknown username (not in the `catalyst_known_admins` lookup).
‚Ä¢ **Compliance:** Archive privileged activity reports for audit compliance (SOX, PCI-DSS, ISO 27001).

Step 5 ‚Äî Troubleshoot
‚Ä¢ **Expected privileged activity not appearing:** The `auditResourceType` values may differ across Catalyst Center versions. Run `| stats count by auditResourceType | sort -count` to identify the exact values used in your environment.
‚Ä¢ **Service account activity flagged as suspicious:** Maintain a `catalyst_service_accounts` lookup and filter service accounts from the human activity dashboard.
‚Ä¢ **IdP role sync generating bulk role changes:** When the IdP syncs roles, many USER/ROLE entries appear simultaneously. Correlate with IdP sync schedules to distinguish from manual changes.
‚Ä¢ **No audit log events arriving:** verify the `audit_logs` input is enabled and check `index=_internal` for API error or endpoint timeout messages from the TA.
‚Ä¢ **Missing fields or empty values:** some audit events from system processes have minimal descriptions; NULL values in `auditDescription` are expected for internal events.
‚Ä¢ **Fewer events than expected:** the audit log API requires SUPER-ADMIN-ROLE; credential access denied errors indicate the service account lacks this role.
grandmaExplanation: We keep a close eye on what your most powerful admin accounts are doing on Catalyst Center, catching anything unusual that could mean trouble.
